report_name: Data-Wiping Attacks on the Rise, Abusing Legitimate Admin Tools

threat_actors.name: Handala Hack Team
threat_actors.description: An Iranian hacktivist group that claimed responsibility for the March 2026 cyberattack against Stryker, where they allegedly used Microsoft Intune to issue remote wipe commands to 80,000 devices.
threat_actors.goals: To cause maximum disruption and data destruction, rather than financial extortion.

attack_patterns.name: Data Destruction
attack_patterns.description: The primary goal of the attack on Stryker was unrecoverable data destruction, using Microsoft Intune to issue remote wipe commands to approximately 80,000 devices.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Accounts
attack_patterns.description: Attackers likely gained initial access through compromised credentials for the stryker.com domain and escalated privileges to Global Administrator status within the Microsoft environment.

attack_patterns.name: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_patterns.description: The attackers abused the legitimate Microsoft Intune MDM platform to issue remote wipe commands, effectively using the OS on the endpoints as a tool for destruction.

attack_patterns.name: Password Spraying
attack_patterns.description: Suggested as a possible method for gaining initial access, using one of the 278 previously compromised credentials for the stryker.com domain.

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: Suggested as a possible method for gaining initial access, using one of the 278 previously compromised credentials for the stryker.com domain.

tools.name: Microsoft Intune
tools.description: Legitimate mobile device management (MDM) platform abused by the Handala Hack Team to issue mass remote wipe commands to approximately 80,000 devices at Stryker.

identities.name: Stryker
identities.description: A medical technology company targeted in a data-wiping attack in March 2026, where an estimated 80,000 devices were wiped using Microsoft Intune.

identities.name: Source Logistics
identities.description: A supply chain company that issued a warning on July 22, 2026, about the rising trend of data-wiping attacks, citing the Stryker incident.

identities.name: Outpost24
identities.description: A security research firm that found 278 credentials linked to the stryker.com domain had been compromised prior to the attack.

indicators.name: Intune Remote Device Wipe Command
indicators.pattern:
title: Intune Remote Device Wipe Command
id: 2b8c1f9a-4a3b-4c1d-8e7f-6a5b4c3d2e1f
status: experimental
description: |
  Detects a remote wipe command initiated from Microsoft Intune. While this can be a legitimate administrative action, a large number of these events in a short period is a strong indicator of a destructive 'living off the land' attack, as seen in the Handala Hack Team attack on Stryker.
references:
  - https://www.globenewswire.com/news-release/2026/07/22/3331494/0/en/source-logistics-warns-supply-chain-industry-about-rising-data-wiping-cyberattacks-outlines-layered-defense-strategy.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-07-22
tags:
  - attack.t1485
  - attack.impact
level: high
logsource:
  product: m365
  service: audit.intune
detection:
  selection:
    OperationName: 'Wipe'
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate remote wipes of single devices for decommissioning or loss. Mass occurrences should be treated as critical incidents.
indicators.description: Detects the 'Wipe' event in Microsoft Intune audit logs, which indicates a remote device wipe command was issued. This was the primary destructive action in the Stryker incident.
